<!-- Checks that there are no tab characters in the file. -->
<!-- Accept LF, CR or CRLF to accomodate devs who prefer different line endings -->
<!-- Checks that FIXME is not used in comments.  TODO is preferred. -->
<property name="message" value='TODO is preferred to FIXME."' />
<!-- Checks if a line is too long. -->
<!-- All Java AST specific tests live under TreeWalker module. -->
<!-- Required to allow exception in code style -->
An import statement is redundant if:
* It is a duplicate of another import. This is, when a class is imported more than once.
* The class non-statically imported is from the java.lang package, e.g. importing java.lang.String.
* The class non-statically imported is from the same package as the current package.
An import statement is unused if:
It's not referenced in the file.

<!-- Validates non-private, static, final fields against the expression "^[A-Z][A-Z0-9]*(_[A-Z0-9]+)*$". -->
value="Variable ''{0}'' should be in ALL_CAPS (if it is a constant) or be private (otherwise)."/>
<!-- Validates static, non-final fields against the supplied expression. -->
<!-- Validates non-static members against the supplied expression. -->
<!-- Validates identifiers for method names against the supplied expression. -->

<!-- Validates identifiers for local final variables against the expression "^[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*$". -->
<!-- Validates identifiers for local variables against the expression "^[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*$". -->

Java style: public static void main(String[] args) // Allowed
C style:    public static void main(String args[]) // Not allowed
<!-- Checks if a catch block is empty and does not contain any comments. -->

<!-- Checks right curlies on CATCH, ELSE, and TRY blocks are on
the same line. e.g., the following example is fine:
<!-- This next example is not fine:
<!-- Checks for braces around loop blocks -->
return 1; // else if should always be multi line
<!-- Checks that each variable declaration is in its own statement and on its own line. -->
<!-- Checks that long constants are defined with an upper ell.-->
<!-- Warn about falling through to the next case statement.  Similar to
javac -Xlint:fallthrough, but the check is suppressed if a single-line comment
on the last non-blank line preceding the fallen-into case contains 'fall through' (or
some other variants which we don't publicized to promote consistency).
value="fall through|Fall through|fallthru|Fallthru|falls through|Falls through|fallthrough|Fallthrough|No break|NO break|no break|continue on"/>
<!-- Checks that the order of at-clauses follows the tagOrder default property value order.
<!-- Checks if the Class and Interface declarations is organized in this order
1. Class (static) variables. Order: public, protected, package level (no access modifier), private.
2. Instance variables. Order: public, protected, package level (no access modifier), private.
<!-- Checks that default is after all cases in a switch statement -->
<!-- Warn if modifier order is inconsistent with JLS3 8.1.1, 8.3.1, and
public, protected, private, abstract, static, final, transient, volatile,

<!-- Checks that Javadoc block tags appear only at the beginning of the line. -->
<!-- Checks that all Javadoc comments start from the second line. -->
<!-- Checks that each line in Javadoc has leading asterisks. -->
<!-- Checks that each non-empty line in Javadoc has whitespace after leading asterisk. -->
<!-- Checks that for block tags, indentation of continuation lines is at least 4 spaces. -->

<property name="allowedAnnotations" value="Override, Test, BeforeAll, BeforeEach, AfterAll, AfterEach, Subscribe"/>

A CLI-based Stock Keeping Unit (sku) Ticketing System. A localized command-line tool designed to handle inventory specific actions required for individual item SKUs such as damage checks, expiry reviews & quality control. Unlike standard commercial inventory software that tracks quantity, ItemTracker tracks accountability & actions, allowing managers to attach specific tasks with priorities to individual SKUs.
* Represents an error when attempting to add an SKU that already exists in the system.
* Constructs a DuplicateSKUException with the conflicting SKU ID.
* @param skuId The ID of the SKU that caused the conflict.
super("An SKU with the ID '" + skuId + "' already exists in the warehouse.");
* Represents an error when an operation requires items but the target list is empty.
* Constructs an EmptyListException specifying which list is empty.
* @param listType A description of the empty list (e.g., "warehouse SKU list" or "task list").
super("The " + listType + " is empty. Please add items first.");
* Represents an error when a user command is unrecognized, malformed, or has extra arguments.
* Constructs an InvalidCommandException with a specific explanation.
* @param message The detail message explaining the syntax error or unknown command.
* Represents an error when a provided date string does not conform to the expected format.
* Constructs an InvalidDateException with the invalid date string.
* @param invalidDate The invalid date string provided by the user.
super("Invalid date format: '" + invalidDate + "'. Please use YYYY-MM-DD format.");
* Represents an error when a provided index is either out of bounds or not a valid integer.
* Constructs an InvalidIndexException for a non-numeric format error.
* @param invalidInput The string input that failed to parse as an integer.
super("Invalid index format: '" + invalidInput + "'. Please use a positive number.");
* Constructs an InvalidIndexException for an out-of-bounds error.
* @param max The maximum valid index allowed.
* @param isTask True if the index refers to a task, false if it refers to an SKU.
? "Index out of range. There are no " + (isTask ? "tasks" : "SKUs") + " available."
: "Index out of range. Please use a number between 1 and " + max + ".");
* Represents the base exception for all ItemTasker-specific errors.
* All custom exceptions in the application should inherit from this class.
* Constructs an ItemTaskerException with the specified detail message.
* @param message The detail message describing the error.
* Constructs an ItemTaskerException with the specified detail message and cause.
* @param message The detail message describing the error.
* @param cause The underlying cause of the exception.
* Represents an error when a required argument (like an index or description) is missing.
* Constructs a MissingArgumentException with a specified detail message.
* @param message The detail message explaining which argument is missing.
* Represents an error when a requested SKU cannot be found in the warehouse.
* Constructs an SKUNotFoundException with the missing SKU ID.
* @param skuId The ID of the SKU that could not be found.
super("SKU '" + skuId + "' not found. Use 'listtasks' to see available SKUs.");
* Represents an error that occurs during file read/write operations or data corruption.
* Constructs a StorageException with the specified detail message.
* @param message The detail message explaining the file or parsing error.
* Constructs a StorageException with the specified detail message and cause.
* @param message The detail message explaining the file or parsing error.
* @param cause The underlying cause of the exception.
* Represents an error regarding the completion status of an SKU task.
* Constructs a TaskStatusException with a specific error message.
* @param message The error message (e.g., "This task is already marked as done.").
* Represents the specific sectors within the warehouse grid.
* Represents a Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) in the inventory ticketing system.
* Contains the SKU identifier, its physical location in the warehouse, and a list of tasks assigned to it.
* Initializes a new SKU with the specified ID and location.
* An empty task list is automatically created for the SKU upon instantiation.
* @param skuID The unique alphanumeric identifier for the SKU.
* @param skuLocation The physical sector location of the SKU in the warehouse.
assert this.skuID != null && !this.skuID.trim().isEmpty() : "SKU ID cannot be null or empty";
assert this.skuLocation != null : "SKU Location cannot be null";
* Retrieves the identifier of the SKU.
* Retrieves the physical warehouse location of the SKU.
* @return The Location enum representing the SKU's placement.
* Retrieves the list of tasks assigned to this SKU.
* @return The SKUTaskList containing all tasks associated with the SKU.
* Represents a list of Stock Keeping Units (SKUs).
* Provides operations to manage the collection of SKUs, such as adding and deleting.
* Initializes an empty list to store SKUs.
* Retrieves the total number of SKUs currently in the list.
* @return The integer count of SKUs being tracked.
* Checks whether the SKU list is completely empty.
* @return True if there are no SKUs in the list, false otherwise.
* Adds a new SKU with the specified ID and location to the list.
* @param skuID The unique alphanumeric identifier for the new SKU.
* @param skuLocation The physical sector location of the new SKU.
public void addSKU(String skuID, Location skuLocation) {
assert skuList.size() > 0 : "SKUList should have size > 0 after adding an SKU";
* Removes the SKU from the list that match the specified SKU ID.
* @param skuID The unique alphanumeric identifier of the SKU to be removed.
* Retrieves the entire list of SKUs.
* @return The ArrayList containing all tracked SKU objects.
* Represents the priority levels of a SKU task.
* Represents a task associated with a Stock Keeping Unit (SKU).
* Each task has an ID, a priority level, a due date, and a completion status.
* Constructs a new SKUTask with the specified ID, priority, and due date.
* @param skuTaskID The unique identifier for this task.
* @param priority  The priority level of this task.
* @param dueDate   The due date of this task.
public SKUTask(String skuTaskID, Priority priority, String dueDate) {
* Constructs a new SKUTask with the specified ID and due date.
* @param skuTaskID The unique identifier for this task.
* @param dueDate   The due date of this task.
* Returns the unique identifier of this task.
* Returns the priority level of this task.
* Returns the due date of this task.
* Returns whether this task is marked as done.
* @return True if the task is completed, false otherwise.
assert !isDone : "Task is already marked as done.";
* Marks this task as not done.
assert isDone : "Task is already marked as not done.";
* Returns a string representation of the task including its status, ID, priority, and due date.
* @return Formatted string of the task details.
return status + " ID: " + skuTaskID + " | Priority: " + priority + " | Due: " + dueDate;

* Handles persistent storage of the warehouse state by serializing and

* @param skuList The list of all SKUs currently in the warehouse.
* @param taskMap A mapping of SKU IDs to their respective task lists.

* @param taskMap The task map to populate with the saved task lists.
public static void loadState(SKUList skuList, HashMap<String, SKUTaskList> taskMap) {

* Main entry-point for the java.duke.ItemTasker application.

* Represents a fully parsed user command, containing:

*
  • A map of flag keys to values (e.g. {@code "n" -> "COMPUTER-MOUSE-X1"})
* @param commandWord The command keyword in lowercase.
* @param args        Map of flag keys (without slash) to their string values.
public ParsedCommand(String commandWord, Map<String, String> args) {
* Returns the value for the given flag key, or {@code null} if not present.
* Returns whether the given flag key is present in this command.
* Parses raw user input into structured commands.

public static ParsedCommand parse(String input) throws InvalidCommandException, MissingArgumentException {
assert input != null : "Input Should not be null";
* Parses a string of flags into a key-value map.

private static Map<String, String> parseFlags(String argsString) throws InvalidCommandException {
// Split at boundaries like " n/" " p/" " d/" " l/" " i/"
* Bundles an SKU task with its parent SKU ID and precomputed Manhattan distance.
* This class acts as a data transfer object to facilitate the sorting of tasks
* based on their physical distance from a target location in the warehouse.
* Constructs a TaskEntry with the specified SKU ID, task, and precomputed distance.
* @param skuId The unique identifier of the parent SKU.
* @param task The task associated with the SKU.
* @param distance The calculated Manhattan distance from the starting location.
TaskEntry(String skuId, SKUTask task, int distance) {
* Handles all console input and output for ItemTasker.
* Centralises printing so that formatting changes only need to be made here.
* Reads and returns the next line of user input, trimmed.
/** Prints the welcome banner on application startup. */
System.out.println(" ItemTasker - CLI SKU Ticketing System");
System.out.println(" Type a command to get started. Type 'help' for a list of commands.");
/** Prints the goodbye message on application exit. */
System.out.println(" Goodbye! All tasks have been saved.");
* Prints a success message prefixed with [OK].
* Prints an error message prefixed with [ERROR].
* Prints an informational message prefixed with [INFO].
* Prints a message when the command entered is not recognised.
System.out.println(" [ERROR] Unknown command: '" + command + "'. Type 'help' for valid commands.");
/** Prints the help reference listing all valid commands and their formats. */
System.out.println("   addsku n/SKU_ID l/LOCATION          Add a SKU to the warehouse.");
System.out.println("   deletesku n/SKU_ID                  Remove a SKU and all its tasks.");
System.out.println("   addskutask n/SKU_ID d/DUE_DATE      Add a HIGH priority task (default).");
System.out.println("         [p/PRIORITY]                  Optional: HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW.");
System.out.println("   deletetask n/SKU_ID i/TASK_INDEX    Delete task at given index.");
System.out.println("   marktask n/SKU_ID i/TASK_INDEX      Mark a task as completed.");
System.out.println("   unmarktask n/SKU_ID i/TASK_INDEX    Unmark a completed task.");
System.out.println("   listtasks n/SKU_ID                  List tasks for a specific SKU.");
System.out.println("   listtasks p/PRIORITY                List tasks filtered by priority.");
System.out.println("   listtasks l/LOCATION                List tasks sorted by distance.");
System.out.println("   help                                Show this help message.");
System.out.println(" Locations: A1 A2 A3 | B1 B2 B3 | C1 C2 C3  (3x3 warehouse grid)");
// Initialize a fresh list before each test
// Verify the remaining SKU is the correct one
// Attempting to delete an ID that doesn't exist
// The list size should remain exactly the same
assertNotNull(sku.getSKUTaskList(), "Task list should be initialized upon SKU creation.");
assertTrue(sku.getSKUTaskList().isEmpty(), "Newly created SKU should have an empty task list.");
String expected = "[ ] ID: SKU-P1 | Priority: HIGH | Due: 2026-04-01";
// Setup Pallet A at A1 (0,0) with 2 tasks
// Setup Pallet B at B1 (1,0) with 1 task
// A1 is at (0,0). PALLET-A is at A1 (dist 0), PALLET-B is at B1 (dist 1)
// PALLET-B is at B1. Distance from A1 to B1 is |0-1| + |0-0| = 1
// Distance from C3 (2,2) to B1 (1,0) is |2-1| + |2-0| = 3
